title: IRS Code 535 Meaning (Bankruptcy Terminated) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 535 reflects bankruptcy termination activity. Learn what termination typically signals for collection status, how it differs from dismissal or discharge, and what to verify before advising clients.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 535 Meaning

IRS Code 535 generally indicates bankruptcy termination in IRS records. This page explains what termination typically signals, how it differs from dismissal or discharge, and what to verify before advising clients on collection posture and next steps.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 535?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 535 reflects bankruptcy termination activity on the tax module. It signals that the bankruptcy proceeding has been terminated in IRS systems.

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 520, 531, 532
Typical context
End of bankruptcy lifecycle in IRS records
Client-friendly summary
"Your bankruptcy case shows as terminated"

When does Code 535 appear?

  • After termination is recorded: The IRS posts 535 when the bankruptcy proceeding is marked terminated in its records.
  • Following prior bankruptcy activity: Often appears after filing (520) and dismissal (531) or closure (532).
  • When lifecycle status changes: Indicates a procedural end reflected in IRS systems.

What does Code 535 usually signal?

Bankruptcy protections likely ended

Termination often means the bankruptcy is no longer active in IRS systems. Confirm whether the automatic stay has lifted and whether normal collection processes may resume.

Review discharge vs dismissal context

Termination does not itself confirm discharge. Review the full sequence of codes to determine whether debts were discharged, dismissed, or simply administratively closed.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Review the full bankruptcy timeline: Check 520, 530, 531, 532 entries.
  2. Confirm enforcement posture: Verify whether collection actions can resume.
  3. Document client impact: Prepare a written explanation outlining status and next planning steps.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Bankruptcy lifecycle
Code 520 – Bankruptcy filed
Code 531 – Bankruptcy dismissed
Code 535 – Bankruptcy terminated

FAQ

What does IRS Code 535 mean on an IRS transcript?

It indicates bankruptcy termination activity has been recorded for the module.

How is Code 535 different from dismissal?

Dismissal (531) reflects a specific procedural outcome, while 535 reflects termination status in IRS systems. Review both entries for context.

Does Code 535 mean collections resume?

Often termination removes bankruptcy protections, but confirm current legal posture before advising clients.

What related transcript codes should I review?

Review codes 520, 530, 531, and 532 to understand the full bankruptcy lifecycle.
